n1.speaker: narrator
n1.line: Nightfall at the riverbank. Gary settles into the reeds to watch the watching-spot. Ferris Mott's idea of stealth arrives ten minutes later, humming.

n2.speaker: narrator
n2.line: In the dark, a rustle of shared rations. A pause. An offering, extended weasel-to-gibbon across the reeds:

n4.speaker: narrator
n4.line: The longest silence in the history of Lanternside journalism.

n6.speaker: narrator
n6.line: By the boathouse fence: digging. Hole number six is underway when a shape ambles out of the dark, eating a pastry with the unhurried confidence of the law.

n7.speaker: tuck
n7.line: Evening, Mott. That'd be hole number six.

n8.speaker: ferris
n8.line: Ferris Mott can explain everything using this map!

n9.speaker: prudence
n9.line: That is MY map. That is an 1890s survey document and it has JAM on it.

n10.speaker: narrator
n10.line: A crowd gathers, nightclothes and lanterns. The town exhales: solved! Then Tuck upends the sack — trowels, jam, maps, one bent curtain rail. No capsule. No contents. The exhale curdles.

n11.speaker: tuck
n11.line: Wrong crimes, folks. Right weasel, wrong crimes.
